sentence_text: I spent more than 10 years at Bank Islam and found the benefits to be quite comprehensive.
review_body_text: I spent more than 10 years at Bank Islam and found the benefits to be quite comprehensive. The work culture is generally positive with supportive and fun colleagues, and the bank offers good flexibility through work-from-home arrangements for specific departments. However, there are significant challenges regarding work-life balance, particularly in operations where the workload is often overwhelming. Since 2021, the bonus payouts (averaging 2 months) have not felt commensurate with the level of effort required. Furthermore, the promotion structure appears to be heavily influenced by seniority and favoritism rather than clear meritocracy, which can be discouraging for high-performers. Additionally, top management often feels disconnected from ground-level operational reality. While it is a stable organization, the compensation-to-effort ratio and career progression transparency need improvement.
review_title: Comprehensive benefits with concerns over operational balance and promotion fairness

sentence_text: My experience working here has been largely positive, with a supportive environment that encourages learning and growth.
review_body_text: My experience working here has been largely positive, with a supportive environment that encourages learning and growth. The team is friendly, approachable, and always willing to help when needed. I appreciate the collaborative culture, where ideas are welcomed and everyone’s input is valued. The management team is generally understanding and open to feedback, creating a comfortable space to discuss challenges and improvements. The workplace also provides good opportunities to develop new skills, whether through hands-on tasks or guidance from more experienced colleagues. The work processes are quite organized, which helps in managing tasks efficiently. While there is still room for improvement in terms of workload balance and clearer communication at times, the overall atmosphere remains motivating.

sentence_text: My internship at Maybank was an incredibly valuable experience.
review_body_text: My internship at Maybank was an incredibly valuable experience. I learned how a leading financial institution operates and gained hands-on exposure to real banking tasks. The team was supportive and always willing to guide me, which helped me develop both technical and communication skills. I was involved in data entry, customer service, and administrative work, which improved my attention to detail and time management. The working environment was professional yet friendly, making it easy to adapt and learn. Overall, my internship at Maybank gave me a strong foundation and clearer understanding of the banking industry.

sentence_text: What is the best part of working at the company?
review_body_text: What is the best part of working at the company? The best part of working at RHB was the supportive work environment and strong teamwork among colleagues. The management encouraged growth, provided continuous training, and valued employees’ contributions, making it a great place to build a long term career. What is the most stressful part about working at the company? As with any banking role, there were moments of pressure during peak periods or when meeting targets, but the management and team support made it manageable. The challenges helped me build resilience and improve my time management skills. What is the work environment and culture like at the company? The work culture was friendly, supportive, and team driven. Everyone worked together towards common goals, and the management valued hard work and integrity. What is a typical day like for you at the company? Every day was focused on serving clients, achieving sales goals, and supporting the team. It was a busy but enjoyable routine that kept me motivated and constantly learning.

sentence_text: '- I always saw my colleagues were always working during their annual leaves and weekends in the office for the whole day due to strict deadlines set by ISO regulations - My mentor even asked me to cancel my annual leaves to complete my workloads, and my mentor asked me to complete my workloads during my annual leaves, weekends and public holiday despite unrealistic goals set by the boss and the team.
cons_text: No work life balance, toxic & poor management
review_body_text: '- I always saw my colleagues were always working during their annual leaves and weekends in the office for the whole day due to strict deadlines set by ISO regulations - My mentor even asked me to cancel my annual leaves to complete my workloads, and my mentor asked me to complete my workloads during my annual leaves, weekends and public holiday despite unrealistic goals set by the boss and the team. - Your workload will be increased without letting you know in advance or without asking your permission to do so. - Your colleagues will punish you with extra workloads if you didn't complete their KPI (number of cases to be completed per day) - Even if you have a little progress within 4 months, the boss and my mentor won't be happy or celebrate your progress, the only thing they do is by forcing you to do faster with increasing amount of workloads. - My mentor has very biased treatment, my mentor is always friendly towards the other colleagues (who are close to my mentor), but when comes to my male colleague (in same department) and me as a new worker, my mentor always give very bad compliment or judge us in a biased way. My mentor also reacted excessively on trivial things about me and controlled me on every movement ("Don't show that you are not happy, because I saw you that you banged the table with documents", while the actual event: The documents are too heavy that I can't carry properly, hence I have to arrange the documents with accidental bang). My mentor also talked bad about male colleague behind his back when male colleague took MC due to his illness.
review_title: Very heavy workloads, no work life balance & toxic management

My mentor also reacted excessively on trivial things about me and controlled me on every movement ("Don't show that you are not happy, because I saw you that you banged the table with documents", while the actual event: The documents are too heavy that I can't carry properly, hence I have to arrange the documents with accidental bang). My mentor also talked bad about male colleague behind his back when male colleague took MC due to his illness.
review_title: Very heavy workloads, no work life balance & toxic management

pros_text: Learn to work independently. Personal growth through self-initiative.
review_body_text: This review is for most positions under the Deposit Department: Poor management, no proper space to meet clients, and zero guidance. Had to self-learn everything. Toxic culture—colleagues fought over customers, and superiors claimed credit for our hard work and performance. Stressful job with little support or recognition. High targets, but performance not fairly recognised. The most enjoyable part was achieving targets through my own effort, despite the lack of support. It gave me a sense of personal accomplishment, even if it wasn’t recognised by management.

sentence_text: A typical day was fast-paced, handling high volumes of customer issues with tight performance targets.
review_body_text: A typical day was fast-paced, handling high volumes of customer issues with tight performance targets. I learned to manage pressure, improve my communication skills, and adapt quickly especially after the Citi Bank migration. Unfortunately, management was unresponsive and showed little concern for employee well-being. The culture was heavily politicized, with favoritism and internal politics often overshadowing merit and hard work. There was no work-life balance, and internal politics were overwhelming. Citi staff were treated poorly after the transition, which made things even harder. The hardest part was staying motivated in a toxic environment. The best part was helping customers and resolving their issues—it gave a real sense of accomplishment. Overall, not a supportive place to grow long-term.
review_title: While the job offered some valuable learning experiences, the overall environment at UOB Bank—particularly post-Citi Bank migration—was demoralizing.

sentence_text: My internship experience was okay overall.
review_body_text: My internship experience was okay overall. It really depends on which department you’re placed in—if you end up in the right one, it’s easier to fit in and there’s a better chance of getting absorbed into the company. The allowance was given based on the number of days you worked, which might not work for everyone expecting a fixed monthly pay. Honestly the people in my team are nice, but in terms of workloads there weren’t much that interns are allowed to do as it is customer’s privacy matters.

sentence_text: I am currently working there as a Management Trainee, i find it even in a week it is too overwhelming.
review_body_text: I am currently working there as a Management Trainee, i find it even in a week it is too overwhelming. The management is on another whole different level, they tend to stick with the paper based era whereas people are going towards technologies. I would say run while you can, there is no good employee welfare, pantry is provided but not with food, works are expected to be learnt in one go despite learning phase. Superiors are just not very welcoming, there is this heavy aura around them. Working here means forgetting of going back on time, the very first day itself the manager told me " here i don't want people to go back on time" usual working time is from 08:45 am to 5:45pm but you are required to work more but none is claimable. Basically speaking no one goes back on time sharp 05:45pm. Overall the management and the way they handle the process of everything is super old school, they still have typewriters to write on vouchers. therefore, explore other firm or field this place is not recommended for the sake of sanity.

sentence_text: Working at Public Bank Berhad can be a rewarding yet demanding experience, especially in the fast-paced environment of a commercial bank.
review_body_text: Working at Public Bank Berhad can be a rewarding yet demanding experience, especially in the fast-paced environment of a commercial bank. Like most banks, the workload can be overwhelming, with employees often expected to handle multiple responsibilities, tight deadlines, and high-performance targets. Long working hours are common, particularly in departments such as sales, credit analysis, and customer service. However, this is considered the norm in the banking industry, where efficiency and accuracy are crucial. One of the key factors that can make the experience more enjoyable is having supportive and competent teammates. A good team can help ease the workload, provide guidance, and create a positive work environment, making it easier to manage stress. Public Bank is known for its structured work culture, and employees who can adapt to its disciplined and customer-focused approach tend to succeed. Overall, while the job can be challenging, it offers stability, career growth opportunities, and valuable banking experience. Employees who thrive under pressure and enjoy teamwork may find Public Bank a good place to build a long-term career.
review_title: A good company to start with in banking line

sentence_text: Poor management and leadership, with a lack of recognition for employees who go above and beyond their regular duties.
review_body_text: Poor management and leadership, with a lack of recognition for employees who go above and beyond their regular duties. During bonus and increment cycles, staff handling only BAU tasks often receive higher total compensation than those who take on additional responsibilities. Moreover, there is a lack of transparency in the compensation distribution process. Favoritism from both leadership and HRBP influences career growth, resulting in the same individuals receiving promotions every 2–3 years, while others are overlooked. Despite implementing 360-degree feedback and the Employee Engagement Survey (EES), employee concerns remain unaddressed. The work environment is highly political and toxic, with poor work-life balance. The recent acquisition of Citi’s business appears to have been poorly managed, leading to a hiring freeze post-merger. As a result, employees are now handling workloads far beyond their capacity, yet compensation remains minimal, with low increments and bonuses. While it is understandable that the bank must demonstrate profitability to shareholders, it is crucial to acknowledge the efforts of employees who drive this success.

sentence_text: During my internship at Bank Rakyat, I had the opportunity to gain practical experience in one of Malaysia's leading cooperative banks.

I was involved in various aspects of banking operations, such as customer service, handling financial products, and administrative support. This experience allowed me to enhance my communication, teamwork, and problem-solving skills while learning from experienced professionals. It provided me with valuable insights into the cooperative banking sector and contributed significantly to my personal and professional development.

sentence_text: Great Work Environment: Employees often mention the inclusive and collaborative culture at Maybank.
review_body_text: Great Work Environment: Employees often mention the inclusive and collaborative culture at Maybank. There is a strong sense of teamwork, and people enjoy working in a diverse, respectful, and supportive environment. Work-Life Balance: Many reviews highlight the company's efforts to maintain a good work-life balance. Employees appreciate the flexibility in working hours, the option for remote work (in some roles), and generous leave policies. Career Growth Opportunities: Maybank is known for offering career advancement opportunities. Employees often note that the company invests in their professional development, with various training programs and mentorship opportunities available to support career progression. Employee Benefits: Benefits packages are another strong point. Maybank offers competitive compensation, health insurance, retirement benefits, and wellness programs. Employees value these perks, which contribute to their overall job satisfaction. Leadership and Management: Many employees appreciate the approachable leadership at Maybank, noting that management is supportive and values open communication. The company fosters a culture of feedback, which helps employees feel heard and valued.

The leadership team was approachable, and there were many opportunities to grow and learn within the organization. I especially appreciated the structured training programs and the emphasis on teamwork, which helped me develop valuable skills in financial services and customer relations. Overall, Bank Islam is an excellent place to work if you’re looking for a dynamic, professional, and rewarding workplace

sentence_text: Public sector banks are known for offering stable and attractive salary packages, making them a popular choice for individuals seeking long-term career opportunities.
review_body_text: Public sector banks are known for offering stable and attractive salary packages, making them a popular choice for individuals seeking long-term career opportunities. While the salary structure varies based on the position and level of experience, public bank employees typically enjoy competitive pay scales that are often revised based on recommendations from government pay commissions. A key advantage is the inclusion of various allowances such as housing rent allowance (HRA), dearness allowance (DA), and special allowances. In addition to the fixed salary, public bank employees also receive additional perks like medical benefits, pension schemes, and paid leave. Bonuses and annual increments are also part of the compensation structure, making it financially rewarding over time. The salary in public banks may not be as high as in private sector banks initially, but the long-term financial security, work-life balance, and other benefits make public banks a desirable option for many. Additionally, public bank employees often have more structured career paths, including promotional avenues that lead to higher pay grades and responsibilities.

sentence_text: My time at CIMB has unfortunately been less than satisfactory.
review_body_text: My time at CIMB has unfortunately been less than satisfactory. While there are some positives, such as a supportive team and a convenient location, the overall experience has been marred by significant shortcomings. One of the most glaring issues is the lack of effective management. There seems to be a disconnect between upper management's vision and the day-to-day operations, leading to confusion and a sense of aimlessness among employees. This has resulted in inefficiencies and missed opportunities for improvement. Additionally, the work-life balance at CIMB leaves much to be desired. The workload can be overwhelming, often requiring employees to work long hours with little acknowledgment or compensation. This imbalance has taken a toll on morale and productivity. Furthermore, opportunities for career development are scarce. There is a noticeable absence of structured programs or support for advancing one's skills and career within the company. In conclusion, while there are some positive aspects to working at CIMB, such as a supportive team environment and convenient location, the overall experience has been subpar due to ineffective management, poor work-life balance, and limited career development opportunities. Prospective employees should carefully weigh these factors before considering employment here..

review_title: Not a good place unless you're willing to do anything for money

pros_text: Benefits/Salary for fresh graduates/ OT claims
review_body_text: 1. You need to have communication skills including negotiation, patience, and listening skills to assist the customers with their inquiries outstandingly. Customers are generally normal and sane. Regardless they might rant because they are having difficulties. Just assist, advise, and resolve. 2. Systems are numerous. Getting used to its interface. Your daily tasks may help you to improve how to utilize them. Also, please give ample time to catch up and get used to the systems even though OIC might not assist you many times. Some OICs did good things when they willingly shared notes about raising the service request. You just need to refer. 3. The 1-month training class might not be enough, the notes you can't gather from outside of class. So for those who want to revise, please jot down clearly inside your notebook. Once you hit the floor, you only can refer to their Infohub system or your simplified notes. You try to focus on the necessary topic first. Ask your trainer the most common inquiries about EACH topic. Because trust me, you can't remember 100% from class to call floor later on. 3. OICs might be helping you but they will expect you to work independently, and catch up so fast. Sometimes, they might get irritated when you ask twice because they think they already explained thousands of times. Perhaps the same questions also come from your colleagues. So for those who are not familiar with banking terms/products, if your background is not related to banking/financials, you have to learn yourself. This one is supposed to be okay, but if you need them and they don't entertain willingly, definitely that will let you down. 4. Systems are fine except I only encounter difficulties with R*W**, it needs to be redesigned to a friendlier interface at least for those agents. I think it's too chaotic and tedious. Just need to make it readable so that easier to answer the enquiries instead of arranging a callback because you usually need more time to extract the info. 5. No work-life balance, kindly expect this as you need to perform follow-ups. But, as recommended by OICs/seniors, try to multitask by submitting the SR while talking with customers. Either listen to rants and do your job, or just put them on hold to submit the SR, both have been practiced there. You can choose to claim OT as well at the end of the shift to submit the SR but extra works that you have to get validation from your OICs or TM. Despite this being normal, they needed to check first. 6. I realized that OICs might have different understandings. So well equipped yourself with knowledge, and practiced to rely on many OICs/TMs for the same inquiries. You will notice to get different answers. 7. The job is fun, especially for those who like to talk to people and assist them. I wanted to stay but I felt disappointed for a couple of reasons, which made me resign. But rest assured, if you have REALLY good support and fun colleagues, you actually can survive.

Your daily tasks may help you to improve how to utilize them. Also, please give ample time to catch up and get used to the systems even though OIC might not assist you many times. Some OICs did good things when they willingly shared notes about raising the service request. You just need to refer. 3. The 1-month training class might not be enough, the notes you can't gather from outside of class. So for those who want to revise, please jot down clearly inside your notebook. Once you hit the floor, you only can refer to their Infohub system or your simplified notes. You try to focus on the necessary topic first. Ask your trainer the most common inquiries about EACH topic. Because trust me, you can't remember 100% from class to call floor later on. 3. OICs might be helping you but they will expect you to work independently, and catch up so fast. Sometimes, they might get irritated when you ask twice because they think they already explained thousands of times. Perhaps the same questions also come from your colleagues. So for those who are not familiar with banking terms/products, if your background is not related to banking/financials, you have to learn yourself. This one is supposed to be okay, but if you need them and they don't entertain willingly, definitely that will let you down. 4. Systems are fine except I only encounter difficulties with R*W**, it needs to be redesigned to a friendlier interface at least for those agents. I think it's too chaotic and tedious. Just need to make it readable so that easier to answer the enquiries instead of arranging a callback because you usually need more time to extract the info. 5. No work-life balance, kindly expect this as you need to perform follow-ups. But, as recommended by OICs/seniors, try to multitask by submitting the SR while talking with customers. Either listen to rants and do your job, or just put them on hold to submit the SR, both have been practiced there. You can choose to claim OT as well at the end of the shift to submit the SR but extra works that you have to get validation from your OICs or TM. Despite this being normal, they needed to check first. 6. I realized that OICs might have different understandings. So well equipped yourself with knowledge, and practiced to rely on many OICs/TMs for the same inquiries. You will notice to get different answers. 7. The job is fun, especially for those who like to talk to people and assist them. I wanted to stay but I felt disappointed for a couple of reasons, which made me resign. But rest assured, if you have REALLY good support and fun colleagues, you actually can survive.

Your daily tasks may help you to improve how to utilize them. Also, please give ample time to catch up and get used to the systems even though OIC might not assist you many times. Some OICs did good things when they willingly shared notes about raising the service request. You just need to refer. 3. The 1-month training class might not be enough, the notes you can't gather from outside of class. So for those who want to revise, please jot down clearly inside your notebook. Once you hit the floor, you only can refer to their Infohub system or your simplified notes. You try to focus on the necessary topic first. Ask your trainer the most common inquiries about EACH topic. Because trust me, you can't remember 100% from class to call floor later on. 3. OICs might be helping you but they will expect you to work independently, and catch up so fast. Sometimes, they might get irritated when you ask twice because they think they already explained thousands of times. Perhaps the same questions also come from your colleagues. So for those who are not familiar with banking terms/products, if your background is not related to banking/financials, you have to learn yourself. This one is supposed to be okay, but if you need them and they don't entertain willingly, definitely that will let you down. 4. Systems are fine except I only encounter difficulties with R*W**, it needs to be redesigned to a friendlier interface at least for those agents. I think it's too chaotic and tedious. Just need to make it readable so that easier to answer the enquiries instead of arranging a callback because you usually need more time to extract the info. 5. No work-life balance, kindly expect this as you need to perform follow-ups. But, as recommended by OICs/seniors, try to multitask by submitting the SR while talking with customers. Either listen to rants and do your job, or just put them on hold to submit the SR, both have been practiced there. You can choose to claim OT as well at the end of the shift to submit the SR but extra works that you have to get validation from your OICs or TM. Despite this being normal, they needed to check first. 6. I realized that OICs might have different understandings. So well equipped yourself with knowledge, and practiced to rely on many OICs/TMs for the same inquiries. You will notice to get different answers. 7. The job is fun, especially for those who like to talk to people and assist them. I wanted to stay but I felt disappointed for a couple of reasons, which made me resign. But rest assured, if you have REALLY good support and fun colleagues, you actually can survive.

review_body_text: 1. You need to have communication skills including negotiation, patience, and listening skills to assist the customers with their inquiries outstandingly. Customers are generally normal and sane. Regardless they might rant because they are having difficulties. Just assist, advise, and resolve. 2. Systems are numerous. Getting used to its interface. Your daily tasks may help you to improve how to utilize them. Also, please give ample time to catch up and get used to the systems even though OIC might not assist you many times. Some OICs did good things when they willingly shared notes about raising the service request. You just need to refer. 3. The 1-month training class might not be enough, the notes you can't gather from outside of class. So for those who want to revise, please jot down clearly inside your notebook. Once you hit the floor, you only can refer to their Infohub system or your simplified notes. You try to focus on the necessary topic first. Ask your trainer the most common inquiries about EACH topic. Because trust me, you can't remember 100% from class to call floor later on. 3. OICs might be helping you but they will expect you to work independently, and catch up so fast. Sometimes, they might get irritated when you ask twice because they think they already explained thousands of times. Perhaps the same questions also come from your colleagues. So for those who are not familiar with banking terms/products, if your background is not related to banking/financials, you have to learn yourself. This one is supposed to be okay, but if you need them and they don't entertain willingly, definitely that will let you down. 4. Systems are fine except I only encounter difficulties with R*W**, it needs to be redesigned to a friendlier interface at least for those agents. I think it's too chaotic and tedious. Just need to make it readable so that easier to answer the enquiries instead of arranging a callback because you usually need more time to extract the info. 5. No work-life balance, kindly expect this as you need to perform follow-ups. But, as recommended by OICs/seniors, try to multitask by submitting the SR while talking with customers. Either listen to rants and do your job, or just put them on hold to submit the SR, both have been practiced there. You can choose to claim OT as well at the end of the shift to submit the SR but extra works that you have to get validation from your OICs or TM. Despite this being normal, they needed to check first. 6. I realized that OICs might have different understandings. So well equipped yourself with knowledge, and practiced to rely on many OICs/TMs for the same inquiries. You will notice to get different answers. 7. The job is fun, especially for those who like to talk to people and assist them. I wanted to stay but I felt disappointed for a couple of reasons, which made me resign. But rest assured, if you have REALLY good support and fun colleagues, you actually can survive.

sentence_text: Working at Bank Rakyat as a contract executive for two years was a valuable and enriching experience.
cons_text: The system used by everyone is new and nowhere near perfect.
review_body_text: Working at Bank Rakyat as a contract executive for two years was a valuable and enriching experience. Despite the occasional stress, the knowledge gained in the field of financing was immense. The supportive and friendly nature of my colleagues significantly contributed to making the overall work environment positive. One notable aspect of the job was the yearly bonuses, which were exceptionally good. This not only served as a motivating factor but also reflected the organization's commitment to recognizing and rewarding the hard work of its employees. The acceptable overtime policy allowed for a healthy work-life balance, and the collaborative atmosphere within the bank made the workload more manageable. One of the highlights of my time at Bank Rakyat was the willingness of my colleagues to share their knowledge and expertise. The culture encouraged learning from one another, fostering a sense of teamwork and camaraderie. This collaborative approach helped me to navigate the complexities of the job more effectively and ensured a smoother integration into the organizational dynamics. Overall, my experience at Bank Rakyat was not only professionally rewarding but also personally fulfilling. The combination of a supportive work environment, generous bonuses, and the opportunity to learn from experienced colleagues made it a positive chapter in my career.

sentence_text: Im an intern (3 months) i could give an overall of 3 stars.
review_body_text: Im an intern (3 months) i could give an overall of 3 stars. Enhance the communication skills where most of the colleagues in my team are much older in the range of 40-50. The working enviroment and working hours are much flexible where only need to onsite at least 2days and wfh the other day. Allowance rm1.5k exclude the epf deduction (i wondered is this necessary). Can go buy food or snacks anytime. I think that so much for the pros. Things that really make me feel boring and not enjoying my internship is because i have been assigned to the department/division that is not even 50% related to what i studied and what really sad is they lied on the jobscope (where i accept the offer due to the jobscope provided). Overall, i could say this internship with cimb somehow dont give me any experience that is tightly related to my area of study. I really losing hope. It just a waste of the skills i gain from i studied. Theres no proper training and new exposure in my field of study.

sentence_text: Title: Maybank - A Superb Workplace My experience at Maybank has been truly outstanding, and here's why it stands out as an exceptional workplace: 1.
review_body_text: Title: Maybank - A Superb Workplace My experience at Maybank has been truly outstanding, and here's why it stands out as an exceptional workplace: 1. **Inclusive Culture**: Maybank fosters diversity and inclusion, creating a strong sense of belonging among employees. 2. **Supportive Management**: Maybank's leadership is approachable and values employee feedback. 3. **Work-Life Balance**: The company promotes work-life balance, offering flexibility and wellness support. 4. **Growth Opportunities**: Maybank invests in professional development and career advancement. 5. **Competitive Benefits**: They offer competitive compensation and a comprehensive benefits package. 6. **Innovation**: Maybank embraces innovation and technology, keeping the work dynamic. 7. **Community Engagement**: Maybank encourages community involvement, giving employees a sense of purpose. 8. **Collaborative Environment**: Teamwork is a priority, creating a positive and productive atmosphere. 9. **Modern Facilities**: The workplace is comfortable, modern, and well-equipped. 10. **Job Security**: Maybank provides stability and financial security. In summary, Maybank is a community of passionate individuals working together to achieve common goals. If you seek a workplace that values employees, promotes growth, and makes a positive impact, Maybank is the place to be.

Speaking from my little experience, I do have the product knowledge but that alone is not enough. Support from surrounding is needed too BUT YOU MUST SHOW THE EFFORT. Else, they won't help you. Make sense? Good luck
review_title: Not easy but you can make it

sentence_text: Worked at back office.
review_body_text: Worked at back office. The furniture was old and the bank rarely change the furniture. They will just fix as much as they can. The chair can be dusty and full of suspicious patches but as long as it serves the purpose, they won’t bother to improve. Working there was good in terms of learning, but at one point,if you’re not actively contributing ideas,you won’t stand out. But if you do,can be frustrated as well because the culture there(depends on department) was to promote based on seniority. The management might be the same as other company, could be frustrating dealing with them and okay at the same time. Depends on how you view the situation.
review_title: Typical company you can think of.

sentence_text: Some department have a very well established job segregation.
review_body_text: Some department have a very well established job segregation. However, the team I'm with currently with does not. I have a boss that cannot manage his own workload, not to mention his team. He has the principle as if he is paying everyone's salary. One member of the team gets lower workload than the other. One get heavier than the other. He urges that anyone with heavy workload to work even at night or weekends to finish the tasks and compares to the other if refuse / have any obligations rather than doing so. Also, the group as a whole encouraging hybrid working arrangement. But the dept. Just dream on hybrid arrangement. They loveee to micromanage the staff so no hybrid working exists.
review_title: Very fast paced and depends on your boss for WFH arrangement

sentence_text: Outdated management style.
review_body_text: Outdated management style. Top management inflexible when it comes to opinion from junior. End result is younger talents will likely jump ship while those who just follow orders passively will be rewarded or promoted by the top management. Whilst corporate life do not usually favor the creative minded ppl, rigid management will result in unproductive and unecessary workload at the end of the day. Benefit wise is comparable to other bank. Bonus structure is standard at 2month years end plus up to 4 month performance bonus in a year for normal office job while those in sales can expect double, triple and even more.
review_title: Rigid corporate structure, top to down management style

sentence_text: I was hired with contract because this job is high turnover, no wonder lo.
review_body_text: I was hired with contract because this job is high turnover, no wonder lo. If you didn't not finish the contract time, you will be charge Rm2500 to pay to them as the training bond. The job was so stressing your mind. Everyday have to hear complaints which is the part of the job. If you priorities your mental health, I do not recommend this job. They have the kpi you need to meet, they always keep telling you to shorten the time call but you need to wrap in CRM. It so stressful. If the queries still pending and appeal, you still need to keep on updates where we have many calls that we tend to forget. I got anxiety because always intimidate with the calls. If you handle chat or email, I still consider, but if inbound or outbound calls, think on your mental health. Pros- if you achieve target on that day, they will treat the meal. They provide free beverage vending machine which provide you coffee, cordial and refreshing beverage. You have 1 hour break. Then 30 minutes extra. You have to sacrifice your time because some shift can end at 1am, and start at 6am. Salary is high for fresh graduate which is 2.7k. Your manager is so supportive and understanding, but the call environment too stressful. 4 minutes from lrt ampang Park, which in located at intermark mall.

But everyone was generally nice and friendly.

sentence_text: They hope that you work more than the scheduled work hours and they do not compensate for OT, and the management (Hire Purchase Department) hate to see that their workers go back on time even if you have completed your work you still need to act busy and then go back at 8pm or 9 pm (schedule working hours are 8.45am - 5.45pm).
cons_text: Bad work life balance and overall a bad experience
job_title: Sales and Marketing Executive (Hire Purchase Officer)
pros_text: Any kind of loans, you as a staff will get a lower interest rate
review_body_text: They hope that you work more than the scheduled work hours and they do not compensate for OT, and the management (Hire Purchase Department) hate to see that their workers go back on time even if you have completed your work you still need to act busy and then go back at 8pm or 9 pm (schedule working hours are 8.45am - 5.45pm). If you think that bank benefits are good, you are wrong too, the benefits are all the same with any established companies just the extra thing is that if you work with them you will easily get loans from them, but then again they're pay is not high and the loan benefit is just basically paying back to the people who pays you to work there, and they receive your money with interest, is a win-win situation for them. You will get special housing loan rate with them but if you quit your job your housing interest will increase back to any normal people's interest rate. Bonuses and increment, bonus for HP department is minimum 2 months basic pay, but if you take those 2 months Bonuses and divided by 12 months and you calculate that you work an extra hour every single day, is not even enough to say that that bonuses paid your extra one hour (OT) you worked for that year. Is less then 1.5 times of the OT rate. But considering that they wish you to work until 8 pm, the bonus is not really a bonus anymore if you see it this way, you can be paid more if a company actually pays for OT. They have a performance bonuses is separately paid from the 2 months minimum bonus, this performance bonus can reach up to 6 months pay,only special cases will get 6 month bonuses and this employee actually hit close to 200% or more than 200% of his annual target, the mean average is people achieving their target is around 80% to 100% plus, you will only given this bonus if only you hit more than 100% of your target. Increment is based on KPI and there is no transparency on the calculations of your increments, they always says is up to management to calculate the increment.
review_title: They have a very bad work life balance for any job that is in the Hire Purchase Department unless you are applying for a clerk position

Bonuses and increment, bonus for HP department is minimum 2 months basic pay, but if you take those 2 months Bonuses and divided by 12 months and you calculate that you work an extra hour every single day, is not even enough to say that that bonuses paid your extra one hour (OT) you worked for that year. Is less then 1.5 times of the OT rate. But considering that they wish you to work until 8 pm, the bonus is not really a bonus anymore if you see it this way, you can be paid more if a company actually pays for OT. They have a performance bonuses is separately paid from the 2 months minimum bonus, this performance bonus can reach up to 6 months pay,only special cases will get 6 month bonuses and this employee actually hit close to 200% or more than 200% of his annual target, the mean average is people achieving their target is around 80% to 100% plus, you will only given this bonus if only you hit more than 100% of your target. Increment is based on KPI and there is no transparency on the calculations of your increments, they always says is up to management to calculate the increment.
review_title: They have a very bad work life balance for any job that is in the Hire Purchase Department unless you are applying for a clerk position

Colleagues are all busy with their duties and achieving results leaving very little time for interpersonal interactions. A typical day for Team Lead is packed with projects meeting, discussions leaving very little time to perform paperwork, administrative work, planning and analysis during working hours, as need to work until almost wee hour daily. The hardest part of the job is workload and the most enjoyable part is that you get to learn a lots.
review_title: A challenging workplace with many concurrent projects to manage and daily back to back projects meetings and software applications testing.

sentence_text: Multitasking is a must working in PBB, especially based in Branch.

sentence_text: A typical day at work getting threatened to finish a task that is impossible to finish.
review_body_text: A typical day at work getting threatened to finish a task that is impossible to finish. Management wants unrealistic and unachievable goals when they pay you low and not even pay overtime. Any ideas will not be taken seriously and management will just dismiss them. The salary range is also too low for a company boasting about being in the top 4 bank ranking list in Malaysia and its seemingly high profits. No matter how hard you work, and even if you get high KPI the annual bonus will not be based on the KPI, makes me wonder why is it even there on the first place.
review_title: Not a good place at all

sentence_text: I’am currently serving as a contract clerk in one of the Regional Collection Centre Branches.
cons_text: Certain staffs are lazy, Kaki kipas staffs, Low salary start up for contracted staffs
pros_text: Free lunches (belanja by bosses and senior staffs on a monthly basis), Good staff credit card apporoval limits, Unlimited claims (as long your pengurus cawangan acknowledges it), Staff hotel benefit rates for holidays, Annual Bonuses, Ex-Gratia Payouts, Claimable Medical Bills (certain minor cases), Very religious islamic Staffs and Helpfull staffs
review_body_text: I’am currently serving as a contract clerk in one of the Regional Collection Centre Branches. Yes, the work enviorment is very relaxed becuse 85% of the employees in the office are family people and senior citizens whom have serviced not less than 5 years with the bank. Communication among staffs and management are good but somehow there will be always 1-2 senior staffs whom disagrees everything the boss does or said (very strong in employee rights/unions stuff). Other than that, since its a 100% Malay culture, laziness is there but the act of good in properous meal ‘belanja’ always being done weekly by any of the kind-hearted staffs other than staffs are helpfull among each other. Salary wise for a diploma range contract staff is kind off sad for its figure but atleast the annual bonuses, ex-gratia payouts and claims are elidgable (as per permanent staffs) for service not less than 6 months on the day of the bonus announcement date via email. Even so, in day 2 of reporting, one of the staff recommended me to apply Staff Credit Card (I never had any credit card before) and guess what? They approved for a handsome limit of the card compared to my salary 😂. In conclusion, working with Bank Rakyat is fun and nice place to work just like any other conpanies but the con’s is always there and it depends on how you deal with it professionally.

Product knowledge must always in head and avoid making miscomunicated or wrongly advise to the customer. Process must be fast and most cases must be done in 1 day. This is fast pace working environment, you must process is fast since all the customer need loan as soon as possible. Very challenging and also fun if you can do it. Very recommend to a person who have skills in selling.
review_title: Very challenging and fast working environment

sentence_text: One of the main skills and knowledge that I managed to pick up is the great use of Microsoft Excel for data entry.
review_body_text: One of the main skills and knowledge that I managed to pick up is the great use of Microsoft Excel for data entry. The pay is quite reasonable for a contract employee. The culture is stressful and fast-paced as you are expected to stay back after working hours. The hardest part of the job is bosses having unreasonable expectations of results from daily tasks given. However, one of the things I enjoyed the most is I managed to pick up some valuable skills and made new friends and new connections.
review_title: Stressful and face-paced but somewhat rewarding

sentence_text: Working with this bank has develop me to become more organise and self sufficient.
cons_text: Long hours, Overload work, Lack of manpower
pros_text: Over time pay, Yearly Bonus, Humanity Rights, Medical, Insurance
review_body_text: Working with this bank has develop me to become more organise and self sufficient. It has also made me to prioritize the importance of the task given and to multi task. With the well management and support from the company , it has enable me to work whiles making sure I have time for my family. With a well diverse country, I work alongside with many diversified colleagues and learned about the many cultures we have. The hardest part of my job would be at times due to the multi culture , I would have to put in extra hours during the festive time and working for a Country Head of the Bank , can be demanding at times. The best and most enjoyable part of the job is that I get to experience good food, great friends and fantastic opportunity to travel !
review_title: Good experience with a good team work foundation

review_body_text: I have learned a lot of new knowledge while working here, especially on how banking organization works. I learn about how to deal with customers properly, what we should reacts when facing cranky customers, and this position teach me about how to be a patience person. In term of working facilities, it is very good. All prominent things are provided such as there are complete equipment in pantry rest room also provided. The working culture is superb because everyone focus on their task because they have their target that the should be done daily and we always stay back after normal working hour everyday. Moreover, the hardest part of this job is we need to push customers to pay their debt and the do not have the money and we have to come up with the solutions for them. the most enjoyable part is by the time we hit the target and we can enjoy the incentives.

sentence_text: During my tenure at Public Bank, I am part of Hire Purchase Credit Processing HP Centre.
location: Public Bank Taman Molek HP Centre JB
pros_text: Medical Benefits, Staff Housing Loan, Bonus/Ex-gratia
review_body_text: During my tenure at Public Bank, I am part of Hire Purchase Credit Processing HP Centre. With team of 10 Officers, each of us need to process an average of 10 cases per day and was given 3 days to submit a decision whether the customer submission approved or rejected. It's very stressful as the pace of new case submission was very fast and It need me to go back home late everyday. Different department has different challenges. Like Housing Loan Department are less stressful as officer part only on customer credit evaluation. The rest will be done by panel lawyer as it involves government agencies. For Frontline Counter they are facing Queue Management System (QMS) which HQ will track how many customers are waiting for their number to be call in more than 2 minutes. But the best part in Public Bank, of course the bonus and ex-gratia. The management are good in taking care staff welfare. Medical benefit are given to staff and their families, 2 months bonus are given to all staff every year and in addition special ex-gratia based on staff performance up to 5 months and low interest rate 0% - 2% for staff housing loan. Overall, although work environment are stressful but working in Public Bank build my personality in terms of work wise fast and accurate, customer handling and evaluation, useful experience, etc
review_title: Stressful work environment but the reward/benefits given are great

review_body_text: During my tenure at Public Bank, I am part of Hire Purchase Credit Processing HP Centre. With team of 10 Officers, each of us need to process an average of 10 cases per day and was given 3 days to submit a decision whether the customer submission approved or rejected. It's very stressful as the pace of new case submission was very fast and It need me to go back home late everyday. Different department has different challenges. Like Housing Loan Department are less stressful as officer part only on customer credit evaluation. The rest will be done by panel lawyer as it involves government agencies. For Frontline Counter they are facing Queue Management System (QMS) which HQ will track how many customers are waiting for their number to be call in more than 2 minutes. But the best part in Public Bank, of course the bonus and ex-gratia. The management are good in taking care staff welfare. Medical benefit are given to staff and their families, 2 months bonus are given to all staff every year and in addition special ex-gratia based on staff performance up to 5 months and low interest rate 0% - 2% for staff housing loan. Overall, although work environment are stressful but working in Public Bank build my personality in terms of work wise fast and accurate, customer handling and evaluation, useful experience, etc
review_title: Stressful work environment but the reward/benefits given are great

sentence_text: I gave my entire career working upwards from a graduate trainee till the Senior Vice President level.
job_title: Senior Vice President, Head of Loan Recovery
review_body_text: I gave my entire career working upwards from a graduate trainee till the Senior Vice President level. I was forced to accept a golden handshake not because i was incompetent to lead a strong Loan Recovery team, my unquestionable integrity didn't sit well with one vocal shareholder who also a board of director member. As Head of Recovery, our task to maximise recovery and recalcitrant borrowers we have to punish.. i punished one by winding them up which irked this director as i did it on his friend's company. The rest was history..

sentence_text: A typical day at work would be processing ad hoc appraisal reports for new recruits / internal promotions.
cons_text: Boss can be crazy during peak period
pros_text: Great staff benefits, bonuses, no office politics
review_body_text: A typical day at work would be processing ad hoc appraisal reports for new recruits / internal promotions. Also includes handling annual increment, performance bonus and year-end bonus. I learned a lot about handling problematic staff and dealing with higher management people. The workplace culture is pretty traditional - your boss is always right! Hardest part of the job would have to be juggling ~10 tasks (all with the same deadlines!) At the same time, it's also the most enjoyable part of the job - the sense of satisfaction you get when you manage to complete everything on your to-do list.
review_title: Long working hours but love the colleagues

sentence_text: A typical day begins at 8.45 a.m.
review_body_text: A typical day begins at 8.45 a.m. by employees checking into office and signing their name on the register book. An employee would start going through client files and make a list of important appointments and requests in order to establish priorities and enhance workflow efficiency and effectiveness. In between, the employee may initiate or receive communication from the client on matters involving banking services and products. Around lunch hour, depending on the level of activity in the office, the employee could arrange for a lunch meeting with colleagues or clients who are seeking additional facilities or information on term deposit rates. The cycle then repeats until after lunch where the employee continues to review business cases and address various events at they come from both internal and external stakeholder groups.
review_title: Exciting opportunities to interact with various stakeholders

sentence_text: Working in Mortgage Department requireS to do everything fast & accurate.
pros_text: Flexible on lunch time, flexible on clock in & out, annual leave
review_body_text: Working in Mortgage Department requireS to do everything fast & accurate. One tiny mistake will cost a lot to bank, that why need to check & ensure everything is in order before approve the loan documentation before move to next step. Working as teamwork are important in my team, we can share idea, problems & try to solve together. Bonding between team mate are good so that we can reduce the level of stress when we faced high work loads. Management also give rewards such bonuses, promotion, meet & greet with subordinates which made the staffs feel motivated to work harder
review_title: Productive & need to achieve target set by management

pros_text: Flexible on lunch time, flexible on clock in & out, annual leave
review_body_text: Working in Mortgage Department requireS to do everything fast & accurate. One tiny mistake will cost a lot to bank, that why need to check & ensure everything is in order before approve the loan documentation before move to next step. Working as teamwork are important in my team, we can share idea, problems & try to solve together. Bonding between team mate are good so that we can reduce the level of stress when we faced high work loads. Management also give rewards such bonuses, promotion, meet & greet with subordinates which made the staffs feel motivated to work harder
review_title: Productive & need to achieve target set by management

sentence_text: When I was looking for a new job , Rhb was at the top of my list .
cons_text: Working hour more than 8hours & one day off day only
pros_text: Flexible lunch hour and able to claim mileage & phone bill every month
review_body_text: When I was looking for a new job , Rhb was at the top of my list . Rhb provides great opportunities to work on banking sector. I would encourage leadership to continue getting delegating to Director and Manager level employees to avoid slowing things down. We also have work to do to ensure that our sales aren't going dropped. I've had a lot of jobs before that I worked really hard, but didn't feel like I had an impact. I've been able to use my skills to get ahead here. When I've wanted to take on different responsibilities, I've been able to do those with help from my Leadership and teams. I feel like I've been here for a good amount of time and I've had opportunities to advance myself and learn about my potential. management is helpful, the work is interesting and growth is tremendous. I have worked at RHB for almost 6 years, and play the role as a sales executive and promote to be a assistant sales manager. The growth opportunity for these 6 years with the fun, hard working environment, and the ability to make as much money as you want is like no other job I have ever had.
review_title: Productive , team building & enjoy with the commission earned

The growth opportunity for these 6 years with the fun, hard working environment, and the ability to make as much money as you want is like no other job I have ever had.

sentence_text: The work surrounding is good, I mean the employees, the employer and the computer system is good.
cons_text: There are several people there are lazy to work
pros_text: Boss treat all staff lunch every week on friday
review_body_text: The work surrounding is good, I mean the employees, the employer and the computer system is good. I have learnt how to attend customers when they want make a loan, how to prepare all loan's document, how to attract customer to make loan with bank rakyat, how to calculate how much loan that customers want, how to key in in the system and how to blast sms about loan to customers. The hardest job is when customers argue the rate of personal loan or other loan, because they will compare to other bank and then they do not want to make it loan and they will go other bank. We can not make something because we only can pursue and slowly talk about advantage of bank rakyat for example, bank rakyat can approve personal loan in one day and customers will get the money by that day. The quality of process is very effective that we can use as our strong argue when customers want to switch at other bank. Several bank make their loan in 2 weeks. Only bank rakyat can make within 3 hours or one day by typical.
review_title: Productive work place with faster disburse loan

sentence_text: 8 years in public bank has been quite overwhelming for me as a bank executive.
review_body_text: 8 years in public bank has been quite overwhelming for me as a bank executive. I work in the operation department. Specifically in the cheque clearing department. I'm a verifier. I verify all the cheques we received from the branches via data everyday. We start 5 am in the morning and we finish at 2 pm in the evening which I would say the most enjoyable part of the job. This job teaches me to be very skillful in observation, decision making and working as a team.

sentence_text: I have started working at cimb bank as a teller.
review_body_text: I have started working at cimb bank as a teller. I have learned a lot when I working there since 2007 until I left the bank. The hardest part is I need to make sure that every year, I must achieve my kpi and also help my team to achieve branch kpi as well. The most enjoyable part of the job is I love to meet customer when I have appointed to be at customer service. Every single day when I was at customer service department, I have learn how to handle customer complain, I need to handle all related to customers inquiry regarding the banks product and also I need to open account for new customer. The reason I have left the bank because of I took mss offering by the bank on july 2015.

sentence_text: In 1 year experience in Banking Industries, i learned to have a great communication with public.
review_body_text: In 1 year experience in Banking Industries, i learned to have a great communication with public. For the 1st month i have been training at Bank Islam central in Kuala Lumpur. I been training how to process financing document. After that, i have to dedicated myself to find a sales target per days, week, monthly and also year. My job is to find a client for making a personal financing, housing financing and credit card. To find a client for making financing for this era is very adventure because of economics issues. I take this challenges and find a solution how to find client for my sales target. So many marketing planning that i have learned such as direct marketing for awareness, telemarketing communicate with client, follow up, closed client and finally marketing channel. I need to process documentation and proceed to HQ for approval.

sentence_text: On Monday I begin by going over the list from the previous week, setting priorities.
review_body_text: On Monday I begin by going over the list from the previous week, setting priorities. I review the progress of existing projects, then check the calendar to refresh my memory on the upcoming week. I like to meet with my staff early, mid and late week to discuss priorities, check progress, and then prepare for the following week. I want to make sure, as much as possible, that we can all dedicate to tasks that move projects forward, so I make habits out of organizational tasks for speed.”

sentence_text: I am very hardworking.
review_body_text: I am very hardworking. normal work as managers only from 9 am to 6 pm, Monday to Friday. but I always go back later and work from Monday to Saturday for planning strategy and spend the whole work. Before I became a manager, I only sales executive. I make my revered craft in the group and bosses. I received many certificates and trophies are awarded for great achievements. I learned there how the management of bank branches or offices. starting with finding staff, train them to use the system, teach them how to make sales, motivate staff, audit and more. The most difficult thing when sales targets are not achieved because of the facilities that limit. Where we have no system to make sales outside the area. The most interesting thing is I received salaries that were previously beyond my income 3 thousand per month, when in RHB my income is much higher, reaching nearly 10 thousand. but never more than 10 thousand. so I can afford to buy cars and homes. I have achieved many awards there.

sentence_text: Dealing with a lot of people that has different characters and personalities made me learn a lot about my job.
review_body_text: Dealing with a lot of people that has different characters and personalities made me learn a lot about my job. For example, the way I respond to this person may make him/her think I give a good service but the same technique would not work for another person. Thus, I have to constantly observe the customers and entertain to their enquires in the ways that would make them feel good about my service and make them feel they came to search for answers, they got their answers.
review_title: A good place to gain experience

sentence_text: Daily routine in Public Bank is to evaluate customers' profile and liaise with different branches to get more customers' information before making lending decision.
review_body_text: Daily routine in Public Bank is to evaluate customers' profile and liaise with different branches to get more customers' information before making lending decision. I have learnt to work effectively in a fast paced working environment as there is always deadline in submitting reports. I have also learnt to communicate effectively with all the branches in Malaysia. The management of the company is very centralized and systematic. Co-workers are friendly and we helped each other while working. The most enjoyable part of the job is when challenges happens such as when customers' provided fraud documents, it is our job to detect the fraud and when there is new systems to be tested.

sentence_text: The best experience while working in maybank when our KPI performance is either on target or exceed target.
cons_text: Management didn't bother their staffs who suffering with internal political issues
pros_text: Good to work in permanently because of HR benefits
review_body_text: The best experience while working in maybank when our KPI performance is either on target or exceed target. Meaning we delivered our tasks in timely manner or performance rating could be better than higher management expectation. The reason why bonus will be paid based on rating percentage and key performance in our KPI. Our employer never failed to pay bonus to entire staff except for those who didn't perform well. In additional, we also will be getting an ESOS, equity shareholders. Meaning all Maybankers beneficial will be entitled an ESOS offer depends on their level or position. The higher position is the better ESOS offered. Medical benefits is the most important and the most valuable HR benefit for Maybankers. It will covers a staff and also a family members where it won't be as same as with any other sectors of financial institution or company locally.

review_body_text: The best experience while working in maybank when our KPI performance is either on target or exceed target. Meaning we delivered our tasks in timely manner or performance rating could be better than higher management expectation. The reason why bonus will be paid based on rating percentage and key performance in our KPI. Our employer never failed to pay bonus to entire staff except for those who didn't perform well. In additional, we also will be getting an ESOS, equity shareholders. Meaning all Maybankers beneficial will be entitled an ESOS offer depends on their level or position. The higher position is the better ESOS offered. Medical benefits is the most important and the most valuable HR benefit for Maybankers. It will covers a staff and also a family members where it won't be as same as with any other sectors of financial institution or company locally.

sentence_text: '-working on shift with 5 working days and 2 rest days.
pros_text: provide annual leave, transport, meal allowances and public holiday allowances.
review_body_text: '-working on shift with 5 working days and 2 rest days. every day must log in systems 9 hours. in 9 hours that includes 1 hour break (meal break) and another 1 hours for auxiliary break (pray,smoking and others) -i learned to handled all kind of customer from simple to complicated problem. but i need to assist that all customer with professional and patient. I need to archive monthly Kpi every month as well. -every agent have their Team Leader(TL) or Team Manager. which is will guide and monitor our work productivity. -everyone knows that customer service is stress job especially with financial institution(bank) but environment and colleagues was help me a lot while working. this company have some event like dinner, hiking, aidilfitri event and other. its make us have good relationship not even with same level person in this company -hardest part in this job was i am working with different from my studies. far from any subject in my courses in university. but all this things only hard in the early of my carrier. i showed and proved that if people get a chance they will struggle and committed on any difficult task. same goes with jobs experiences. without chance we cant get experience -lastly, most enjoyable part for sure was satisfaction from customer after can settled and solved problem. include compliment from cust.
review_title: good environment and can enhance carrier growth

sentence_text: I ve started my career in maybank as a clerk.
review_body_text: I ve started my career in maybank as a clerk. I growth greatly in maybank . I see a lots of tranformation . I earn so much benefit during my day in maybank. For me, the best employer in malaysia is maybank. I felt regret leaving mahbank in year 2015 where I insist to take a challlenge become a manager in rhb. But in maybank... the promotion is limited for each branch every year . So if you want something fastrack for career, you definately wont easily get . As many of senior staff waiting for long.

sentence_text: The job scope of sales executive is not only to get a case, develop relationship networks with dealers and customers to market the hire purchase, we need to follow up each stage of the case from the day the case assign until the disbursement of the case.
review_body_text: The job scope of sales executive is not only to get a case, develop relationship networks with dealers and customers to market the hire purchase, we need to follow up each stage of the case from the day the case assign until the disbursement of the case. I have learned how to assign the case, submission to hq, processing (CCRIS, calculation of the interest rate, EIR, DSR, CRR, guarantor and so on), LOU, e-hakmilik, payment, disbursement and so on. The hardest part of the job is to build a new relationship and dealing with dealers and customers. In addition, with the current economics problem, the market was very slow, so quite tough to survive. However, the most enjoyable part of the job is when we can give the best services to our customers and make them satisfied with us.
review_title: Quite tough job however nice team members make us release the stress

sentence_text: Having worked in the Bank for almost 40 years, I would say it was like my second home.
review_body_text: Having worked in the Bank for almost 40 years, I would say it was like my second home. Depending on which unit you work with, it can be very busy and hectic. If I worked in the telecommunication centre where we receive messages from all over the world and having different time zones, I begin my day early and rushing to sort out incoming messages and also very hectic towards end of the day when messages have to go out. We have great teamwork and work with a great sence of urgency with my colleagues. The hardest part was when trade export documents go missing, resulting in importers not being able to get hold of their goods and incurring demurrage charges. It was great to have our co-workers who were helpful and hadworking.

review_body_text: Having worked in the Bank for almost 40 years, I would say it was like my second home. Depending on which unit you work with, it can be very busy and hectic. If I worked in the telecommunication centre where we receive messages from all over the world and having different time zones, I begin my day early and rushing to sort out incoming messages and also very hectic towards end of the day when messages have to go out. We have great teamwork and work with a great sence of urgency with my colleagues. The hardest part was when trade export documents go missing, resulting in importers not being able to get hold of their goods and incurring demurrage charges. It was great to have our co-workers who were helpful and hadworking.

sentence_text: Working for 3 years and 7 months as a banker really provide me with a lot of experience.
review_body_text: Working for 3 years and 7 months as a banker really provide me with a lot of experience. Started my career as BMELP which is a talent management program that I have had passing through for 2 years enable me to adopt a fast learning culture and contributes towards a productive working culture as I was all-rounder for a branch management. After BMELP, I then joined in a more specific sector which is Small and Medium Enterprise (SME) as Business Development Executive (BDE). This role is important because as BDE, I am tasked to meet with customer and offering them financial assistance. This relatively contributes towards the positive growth in Maybank.

sentence_text: Public Bank is no doubt a reputable company with good track record and success.
review_body_text: Public Bank is no doubt a reputable company with good track record and success. All this will not happen without contribution from dedicated staff who strive for the success of the company. As far my long service in Public Bank, I learned a lot to be a good and responsible staff. The most important things are team spirit to ensure all the responsibilities are to be carried out successfully. The Management also is very concern and alert that all opinion and complain from the customers are taken care off. This is one of the reason why Public Bank brand name is quite acceptable to all customers nationwide. All these qualities will be a good example for me to apply when I join new company as well.

sentence_text: Every day was a challenging and exciting day at work since i'm in the sales position in the organisation.
review_body_text: Every day was a challenging and exciting day at work since i'm in the sales position in the organisation. My previous position taught me about customer service, helping me enhancing my personal skills and everything about financial service. The co-workers were helpful. The hardest part of the job is when i need to convincing people to buy the banks product but although it is hard, i took it up as a challenge and it excites me everytime i managed to convince customers to buy the products. The most enjoyable part of the job is when i do felt i am helping these customers to reach their desirable financial goals through ways such as long-term investments and it is enjoyable when i get to assist the banks customers in every way that i can.
review_title: Very good and productive job culture

Motivation and coaching instead of dictatorial leadership should be instilled to create a better working environment. Furthermore, recognition should be given based on performance and merits of the individual NOT otherwise.

sentence_text: No work life balance.
cons_text: Lack of career enhancement opportunity, political issue
review_body_text: No work life balance. When working in banks, you should expect to spend yourself to sacrifice your energy and time to focus on work, to stay back late while little you can get in return. The management is pretty hands off. They'd like to push the staff to finish up a lot of works in a short time by hook or by crook. Co-workers are some team players but some just like to complaint without doing nothing. Working in banking industry is supposed to be the most enjoyable things in life but it becomes miserable especially for the contract staff when the contract is ended without strong and valid reason. Therefore, no career enhancement is promoted in CIMB.

sentence_text: When i was new, most of the sales that i managed to done deal are being eye-ed by colleague.
review_body_text: When i was new, most of the sales that i managed to done deal are being eye-ed by colleague. They will offer you a small amount of money in exchange of giving them the sales. if you refuse, they will never want to guide u in future, and won't even smile at you anymore. This will make u feel uncomfortable, clueless, and unable to hit your monthly sales target. Workload is a lot that you could only get home at 7pm everyday. During peak season, you might be home at 9pm. The good thing is you are not underpaid (what to expect if you have no previous professional working experience), and training provided even though they won't be able to cover on everything in detail. Boss/Manager is really helpful, responsible, kind and friendly.

What I enjoyed the most is the satisfaction from completing a done deal and getting praise and appreciation from customers.

sentence_text: It was an honor to join the first batch of "Manager in Training" (M.I.T) program.
review_body_text: It was an honor to join the first batch of "Manager in Training" (M.I.T) program. It gave chance of exposure to Team manager daily tasks. I had learn how to manage a team efficiently and motivate them to have better performance. Built a strong relationship with fellow colleagues and cooperate to meet customers' issue. It was tough to manage difficult customer but supports always there when needed. I enjoy myself with the kind relationship built between colleagues, but suffer from long hours of follow up for customers' demand, which sometimes not rational, but try to make it happen.
review_title: Things to learn as company still growing

sentence_text: I love and enjoy my working life in Public Bank for the past 28 years and taking this opportunity to thank Public Bank.
cons_text: Too long hours of work. health and family effected
pros_text: 24 hours Guards start work early, attend calls and customers inquir, back logs after office hours & review work on weekends & public holidays
review_body_text: I love and enjoy my working life in Public Bank for the past 28 years and taking this opportunity to thank Public Bank. I like the management style who entrust me to handle the Bills, Remittances,Foreign Currency Current Account and Fixed Deposits, Safe Deposits Box and Chief Cashier SOLELY with only one clerical staff. Month end there are a high numbers of Telegraph Transfer , Demand Draft and Bills to draw down with cut of time. To meet the monthly Figures for loans and Non Interest Incomes. with limited Staff. Manage to increase monthly figures to reach the yearly targets by the month of September to achieve the branch yearly awards.
review_title: Productive with good air conditioning in this hot weather

sentence_text: I came to work early than working time.
cons_text: Solemn working culture especially to new employee
review_body_text: I came to work early than working time. The working period is basically from 8.30 A.M until 5.30 P.M. I had my breakfast at the workplace usually. Literally, I learned something new at the work place every week. I learned about presentation skills, time management, work culture, and the workers different personality. The access card for everyday used need several days for it to be done. The approval for internet access, printing and scanning security slow and required days to be approved and settled. The management kind of blurry and they did not introduce new worker to the existing employees in another department, only among their department and if they have to. Everyone need to know their own colleagues in order to have better working environment. There are friendly co-workers and others rather be in their clique. The hardest part of the job is that there are no work to be done for that particular day, I think a worker must accomplish or contribute something on that day, then he is worthy on receiving the paycheck, because of the work that he had done. The most enjoyable part of the job was when I know what I am doing and receiving work from supervisors or other superiors. This is only the point of view of an intern.

Moreover, we do have our monthly meetings and birthday celebrations once in a month which actually creates a positive bond between everyone.
